summary: Manifest kind-model foundations: a fields-only embedded-locus kind shape (no body slot) and the collection-address kind fact (which manifest, which key path)

edit.description: Add the two read-side kind facts 0021 phase 1 needs: (a) the fields-only shape — an embedded-locus kind with no body slot (distinct from `Content::File`, which still carries verbatim prose), so a registration member has fields and edges but no prose to fill; (b) the collection-address kind fact — which manifest (path) and which key path (`hooks.<Event>`, `mcpServers.*`) a member's registration surfaces at, the manifest's fence. Guard each with the same closed-vocabulary load rejection `Format`/`UnitShape`/`Registration` carry (an unknown value is a load error, never skipped).

`KindFactRow` (line ~1749) gains a collection-address column and the fields-only marker, each a tolerant `#[serde(default)]` round-trip like `registration`/`templates` so the existing built-in rows stay byte-identical (absent = today's file-locus, body-bearing kind). The row round-trips through the lock both directions.

Mirror the two facts on the TS kind surface: the fields-only kind shape (no prose/content in `T`) and the collection-address fact, both erasing to the new `KindFactRow` columns at the seam. The six-fact narration and `KindDefinition` extend, not fork.
Regenerated ts-rs seam binding for the new `KindFactRow` columns (the `sdk/src/generated/` seam home, 36a7662). Build re-runs the seam generator; `tests/seam_bindings_current.rs` gates it current.

tests.asserts: the collection-address kind fact and the fields-only shape round-trip through the lock byte-stably; the existing file-locus built-in rows are unchanged; an unknown collection-address/shape value rejects loud at load

acceptance: A kind declares the embedded fields-only shape and a collection address (hooks.<Event> / mcpServers.*); both facts round-trip through the lock and the SDK erasure, and the closed-vocabulary guard rejects an unknown value.
notes: Phase 1 of 0021 (manifest authoring surface), read-side foundation — hook-first. Fields-only = no body slot (distinct from Content::File). Closed-vocab guard mirrors Format/UnitShape. Foundation for the adapter + the hook/mcp-server kinds; nothing file-locus or body-bearing regresses. scoped at 8998b73

summary: JSON manifest adapter, read face: a JSON-parser peer to frontmatter.rs — declared collection addresses walk into generic extraction, undeclared keys opaque, registration members inferred

new.description: The JSON manifest adapter, read face only — `frontmatter.rs`'s peer. A real JSON parser owns the grammar; a kind's declared collection address selects which key paths walk into the generic surface extractor (`crate::extract`), each entry read as a fields-only registration member; every undeclared key survives as an opaque field. Reading an unrepresented manifest infers its registration members. Write face (canonical whole-manifest regeneration) is phase 2 — NOT this entry; the unrepresented-manifest write stays 0008's `src/json_splice.rs`, untouched.

edit.description: The generic surface extractor gains the collection-address walk: given a manifest's declared address, resolve the key path and extract each entry as a member's fields, reusing the same `Features`/`Primitive` machinery the frontmatter path rides so the soundness boundary stays one boundary.

Discovery/loader dispatch (`discover_kind_units`/`discover_builtin`) routes an embedded manifest kind to the new JSON adapter instead of the frontmatter loader, keyed off the collection-address fact. Idempotence keystone holds — the read is content-derived.

tests.asserts: a JSON manifest with a declared collection address infers one registration member per entry with the declared fields; undeclared keys read back as opaque fields; re-reading an unchanged manifest is idempotent

acceptance: temper reads a JSON manifest via the new adapter: declared collection addresses extract as registration members, undeclared keys survive as opaque fields, and an unrepresented manifest infers its members — read idempotent.
notes: Phase 1 of 0021, read face only (write face is phase 2). Peer of frontmatter.rs — a real JSON parser owns the grammar; the unrepresented-manifest write stays json_splice.rs (0008), untouched. No kind ships in this entry — a test kind exercises the adapter. scoped at 8998b73

summary: The hook built-in kind — embedded, fields-only, collection address hooks.<Event> — with its cited strictest-documented-profile default contract, in the engine std-lib and the SDK provider face

new.description: The hook kind reads a real `.claude/settings.json` `hooks.<Event>` entry as a hook member; its default contract fires on a broken hook and passes a clean one (fixture mirrors the real Claude Code layout, per rust.md).

edit.description: Add the `hook` kind to the embedded std-lib: embedded locus, fields-only shape, collection address `hooks.<Event>`, reusing the `Event` registration channel already carried in `src/kind.rs`. Its field schema is a fetched-and-cited external fact about the Claude Code hooks format (0014).
The embedded built-in lock gains the hook kind's declaration rows (kind fact + default-contract clauses), derived from the module, not hand-transcribed.
Add the `hook` kind value and `hookDefaultContract` (strictest-documented-profile clauses, each with its cite + retrieved date), built with the same `kind<T>()` constructor as the five shipped kinds.
Export `hook` and `hookDefaultContract` from the `@dtmd/temper/claude-code` provider subpath alongside the existing kinds.

Teach `governed_by_any` that the hook kind's collection address governs the `settings.json` hooks segment, so `coverage.unmodeled-surface` stops flagging that segment as checked-by-nothing once the kind lands.
`embedded_declaration_rows()` fixture (line ~72) gains the hook rows so the embedded lock still byte-equals the SDK module's own emit.

tests.asserts: a settings.json hook reads as a hook member; the shipped hook default contract fires on the strictest documented profile and passes clean input; the embedded and SDK-derived built-in locks stay byte-equal

acceptance: temper models a settings.json hook as a hook member; the shipped hook default contract checks the strictest documented profile; the built-in lock (embedded + SDK-derived) stays byte-equal; coverage stops flagging the hooks segment as unmodeled.
notes: Phase 1 of 0021, hook before mcp-server. Reuses the Event registration channel in kind.rs. Build fetches + cites the current Claude Code hooks doc (0014) for the field schema and the strictest-profile default contract. settings.json's full unmodeled-surface retirement (it also holds permissions/opaque keys) waits on phase 2's represented-manifest coverage. Updates the frozen built-in lock, both sides. scoped at 8998b73

summary: The mcp-server built-in kind — embedded, fields-only, collection address mcpServers.* — with its cited strictest-documented-profile default contract, in the engine std-lib and SDK provider

new.description: The mcp-server kind reads a real `.mcp.json` `mcpServers.*` entry as an mcp-server member; its default contract fires on a broken server config and passes a clean one (fixture mirrors the real Claude Code layout).

edit.description: Add the `mcp-server` kind to the embedded std-lib: embedded locus, fields-only shape, collection address `mcpServers.*`. Its field schema is a fetched-and-cited external fact about the Claude Code MCP-server config format (0014).
The embedded built-in lock gains the mcp-server kind's declaration rows (kind fact + default-contract clauses), derived from the module.
Add the `mcpServer` kind value and `mcpServerDefaultContract` (strictest-documented-profile clauses, each cited + dated), built with the same `kind<T>()` constructor.
Export `mcpServer` and `mcpServerDefaultContract` from the `@dtmd/temper/claude-code` provider subpath.
Teach `governed_by_any` that the mcp-server kind's collection address governs `.mcp.json`; `.mcp.json` is wholly this manifest, so its `coverage.unmodeled-surface` finding retires cleanly.
`embedded_declaration_rows()` fixture gains the mcp-server rows so the embedded lock still byte-equals the SDK module's emit.

tests.asserts: an .mcp.json server reads as an mcp-server member; the shipped default contract fires on the strictest documented profile and passes clean input; the built-in locks stay byte-equal; .mcp.json no longer fires coverage.unmodeled-surface

acceptance: temper models an .mcp.json server as an mcp-server member with its cited default contract; the built-in lock stays byte-equal both sides; .mcp.json no longer fires coverage.unmodeled-surface.
notes: Phase 1 of 0021, after HOOK-KIND (shares builtin_kind.rs / builtin_lock.rs / builtins.ts / the frozen lock — serialized, not parallel). Collection address mcpServers.*. Build fetches + cites the current Claude Code MCP-server config doc (0014) for the field schema and strictest-profile default contract. .mcp.json is wholly this manifest, so its coverage finding retires cleanly. scoped at 8998b73

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, public on npm and bumped to 0.1.0 — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP, 543f2fd); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified live at 5717a13: still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
